programme.title: Treasures Decoded
programme.desc: The history of the Sphinx, the Shroud of Turin, the map of El Dorado, and a controversial piece of papyrus.

programme.title: Planet Earth 2
programme.desc: A fascinating natural spectacle.The creators of the program show the wealth of fauna and flora that inhabit the world.They visit all continents, where they observe commonly known and very rare species of animals and plants.Using the development of technology, they show life on Earth from a completely new perspective.They allow you to look at the world through the eyes of the creatures that inhabit it.Cameras placed very close record the normally elusive behavior of wild creatures.High-quality photos allow viewers to be in the very center of events - births, hunts or fights for leadership in the herd.All this is accompanied by commentary by David Attenborough, the famous naturalist, traveler and journalist.The series was divided into six parts.The first one reveals the richness of life on islands - from volcanic ones, through equatorial paradises, to Antarctica.The second episode focuses on mountains, the third - jungles, the fourth about deserts, and the fifth about steppes and savannas.The last one will present the life of animals in cities.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Islands: Nature's Wild Laboratories
programme.desc: The creators of the series take viewers on a journey through the unique ecosystems of islands, from the Arctic to the tropics.The trek includes Borneo, the Okavango Delta, Vancouver Islands, Baffin Island, K'gari and Sri Lanka.

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The creators of the series go on a train journey to various places around the world.During each expedition, they meet people who live in specific areas or work on the most unique railway routes.They will also discover the wonders of nature that can be admired from the train windows.They will talk to engineers responsible for maintaining the lines and historians who will talk about the circumstances of their construction and the difficulties their builders faced.Viewers will be able to admire the landscapes of Norway, New Zealand, Canada, Switzerland, Wales and Spain.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Once Upon A Time In Space
Inside the Factory
programme.desc: The presenters visit the largest factories in the world and reveal the behind-the-scenes of the creation of popular products, normally inaccessible to ordinary consumers.

programme.title: Empire with David Olusoga
programme.desc: British historian David Olusoga tells the story of Britain, examining how a small island became the center of the greatest empire in the history of the world.

programme.title: Mammals - Making Of
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the production of a nature production presenting the life of mammals.The most diverse phylum of the animal kingdom includes both the tiny Etruscan shrew and the blue whale.

programme.title: Eden: Untamed Planet
programme.desc: Documentary filmmakers go to the most remote corners of our planet to discover the secrets of the few untouched ecosystems.From the Namib Desert to Patagonia, only a few places on Earth have remained largely unchanged.Isolated from the rest of the world, they managed to defend themselves against the most harmful effects of human interference.Life there goes on as nature intended.The creators will take viewers on an exciting journey to the last places that are paradise on earth.Amazing, breathtaking landscapes will be presented, as well as exciting behaviors of the most popular animals in the world.Each episode will feature a different unique corner of the globe - from the mysterious, fog-shrouded forests of Borneo to the lush beauty of Africa's Luangwa floodplain The tropical island of Borneo is located in the heart of Southeast Asia.Its area is as large as the US state of Texas, and its forests have remained unchanged for 140 million years.Areas bathed in sunlight are cooled by heavy rainfall.Such greenhouse conditions resulted in the development of approximately 60,000 species of plants and animals there over time.In terms of biology, this island is one of the most diverse.Over 6,000 endemic species have evolved here in isolated conditions.The extraordinary wealth of nature arises from shallow, nutrient-poor soil.However, life managed to triumph here, transforming this place into a unique paradise on earth.

programme.title: Eden: Untamed Planet
programme.desc: Documentary filmmakers go to the most remote corners of our planet to discover the secrets of the few untouched ecosystems.From the Namib Desert to Patagonia, only a few places on Earth have remained largely unchanged.Isolated from the rest of the world, they managed to defend themselves against the most harmful effects of human interference.Life there goes on as nature intended.The creators will take viewers on an exciting journey to the last places that are paradise on earth.Amazing, breathtaking landscapes will be presented, as well as exciting behaviors of the most popular animals in the world.Each episode will feature a different unique corner of the globe - from the mysterious, fog-shrouded forests of Borneo to the lush beauty of Africa's Luangwa floodplain A story about the Namib Desert and the Skeleton Coast.The drought there seems not to be conducive to paradise living conditions.At noon the temperature exceeds 43 degrees Celsius.However, it turns out that this is the only desert inhabited by lions.There are also about 100,000 southern oryxes living here, and nature provides excellent conditions for one of the bird giants.There are numerous ostriches there.The rainfall that appears gives rise to a magical transformation.Seeds that have spent up to 15 years dormant transform the sand into a flowery meadow.This region is also inhabited by about 150 elephants.The Namib is the only desert where, against all odds, they managed to survive thanks to life-sustaining underground waters.

programme.title: Eden: Untamed Planet
programme.desc: Documentary filmmakers go to the most remote corners of our planet to discover the secrets of the few untouched ecosystems.From the Namib Desert to Patagonia, only a few places on Earth have remained largely unchanged.Isolated from the rest of the world, they managed to defend themselves against the most harmful effects of human interference.Life there goes on as nature intended.The creators will take viewers on an exciting journey to the last places that are paradise on earth.Amazing, breathtaking landscapes will be presented, as well as exciting behaviors of the most popular animals in the world.Each episode will feature a different unique corner of the globe - from the mysterious, fog-shrouded forests of Borneo to the lush beauty of Africa's Luangwa floodplain The Luangwa River begins its course in the highlands of Zambia.It flows down to the valley, where it runs through vast, lush savannahs with an area of ​​nearly 70,000 square meters.square kilometers.Eventually it flows into the Zambezi River.During the rainy season, it overflows its banks, keeping this African paradise alive.The local plains then take on a wonderful green color.Leopards are more abundant on the banks of the Luangwa than anywhere else in Africa.During the dry season, pastures dry out and Masai baboons scavenge for grass and insect larvae during the day.As this period enters its crucial phase, Luangwa is visited by brightly colored visitors.Carmine bee-eaters are experts at capturing and eating bees.

programme.title: Once Upon A Time In Space
Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Islands: Nature's Wild Laboratories
programme.desc: The creators of the series take viewers on a journey through the unique ecosystems of islands, from the Arctic to the tropics.The trek includes Borneo, the Okavango Delta, Vancouver Islands, Baffin Island, K'gari and Sri Lanka.

programme.title: The World's Deadliest Weather
programme.desc: Moving accounts of people who survived confrontations with the forces of nature.This series includes, among others:memories of earthquakes in Turkey and Syria and fires raging in Los Angeles.

programme.title: Surgeons: A Matter Of Life Or Death
programme.desc: More stories of life-saving operations in one of the largest hospitals in Great Britain.The series shows up close the activities and work of surgeons with extraordinary skills.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Griff's Canadian Adventure
programme.desc: Welsh comedian and traveler Griff Rhys Jones goes on a journey through Canada, the second largest country in the world.He visits picturesque mountain ranges and vast prairies, as well as metropolises and towns located in the Arctic wilderness.He begins to understand the impact of its rich history on the current state and what determines the originality of individual provinces.Griff visits places in Canada such as Newfoundland on the east coast and Vancouver on the Pacific Ocean.In each episode, the comedian takes on a challenge specific to the region he visits.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Ben Fogle: Return to the Wild 2
programme.desc: Traveler Ben Fogle revisits the participants of the "Life in the Wasteland" program.These people have in common the fact that years ago they decided to abandon their previous existence in favor of living in the bosom of nature, often in a remote area and in extremely harsh conditions.Ben will check, among others:what happened to a former war correspondent who became a grizzly bear expert.He will also revisit a former financier who is now living the life of Tarzan in a swampy area in Georgia.The presenter will find out whether these people are satisfied with their choices.

programme.title: Islands: Nature's Wild Laboratories
programme.desc: The creators of the series take viewers on a journey through the unique ecosystems of islands, from the Arctic to the tropics.The trek includes Borneo, the Okavango Delta, Vancouver Islands, Baffin Island, K'gari and Sri Lanka.

programme.title: Life Below Zero
programme.desc: Another season of the documentary that shows the lives of people living in Alaska who hunt and prepare for the harsh winter.Temperatures in this area drop to -60 degrees Celsius.

programme.title: Jimmy Doherty's New Zealand Escape
programme.desc: Jimmy takes a trip to the tropical north to visit New Zealand.His culinary discoveries will not be the only attraction of the program.There will also be a visit to the winery and a show of seasonal specialties.

programme.title: Mammals - Making Of
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the production of a nature production presenting the life of mammals.The most diverse phylum of the animal kingdom includes both the tiny Etruscan shrew and the blue whale.

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: Everyday objects are prosaic companions of our everyday life and we rarely think about how they were created.However, they often hide fascinating stories.

programme.title: Once Upon A Time In Space
Mammals - Making Of
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the production of a nature production presenting the life of mammals.The most diverse phylum of the animal kingdom includes both the tiny Etruscan shrew and the blue whale.

programme.title: Life Below Zero: Canada
programme.desc: The creators of the series present the everyday life of a group of daredevils struggling with wildlife in various regions of northern Canada during a frosty winter.Johnatan Granier will join the program in the second season.

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The creators of the series go on a train journey to various places around the world.During each expedition, they meet people who live in specific areas or work on the most unique railway routes.They will also discover the wonders of nature that can be admired from the train windows.They will talk to engineers responsible for maintaining the lines and historians who will talk about the circumstances of their construction and the difficulties their builders faced.Viewers will be able to admire the landscapes of Norway, New Zealand, Canada, Switzerland, Wales and Spain.

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of the Atlantic
programme.desc: Actor Martin Clunes continues his journey around the world's islands, this time visiting the Atlantic.There he gets to know the locals, their customs and meets unique animals inhabiting these areas.
